mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over multiple months reflects a deliberate easing cycle that lowers discount rates and supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading month.
failure_condition: The monthly change in FEDFUNDS has a non-negative correlation with forward 20-day SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window, or the mean return in easing periods is not significantly above the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above a critical level signals the onset of a recession, reducing corporate earnings expectations and consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: When UNRATE reaches 4.5%, the forward 60-day SPX return is positive in more than 40% of cases, or the average return is not significantly less than zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising consumer prices and rising unemployment creates a stagflationary environment that compresses corporate profit margins and increases equity risk premiums, resulting in negative forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (CPIAUCSL * UNRATE) in a linear regression predicting 20-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over any 2-year rolling window, or the coefficient has a positive sign.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing bust defined by housing starts falling below a key threshold and continuing to decline, the deepening contraction in residential investment amplifies recession fears and depresses overall economic activity, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1500 and declining, the forward 60-day SPX return is positive in more than 40% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production indicates weakening manufacturing activity and economic contraction, which reduces corporate profits and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The month-over-month change in INDPRO has a non-positive correlation with forward 20-day SPX returns over any 3-year window, or the mean return during declining INDPRO periods is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in real retail sales signals a sudden drop in consumer spending, which foreshadows weaker corporate revenues and a negative market reaction over the following week.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines by more than 1% month-over-month
failure_condition: When RSAFS declines by more than 1% MoM, the forward 5-day SPX return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the average return is not significantly less than zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in both nonfarm payrolls and housing starts signal a synchronized weakening in the labor market and housing sector, indicating broad economic stress that depresses equity market returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: Both PAYEMS and HOUST show negative month-over-month change
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS and HOUST decline MoM, the forward 20-day SPX return is positive in more than 30% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates above a critical threshold represent a tight monetary policy stance that restricts borrowing, investment, and consumption, ultimately weighing on corporate earnings and equity prices over the short term.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, the forward 20-day SPX return is positive in more than 40% of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).
